description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly dark gray to black coloration. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring a mix of jagged edges, flat planes, and some weathered, lighter patches, especially near the base. The rock appears dense and solid, with a few small crevices and chips along its edges, suggesting natural fracturing. Its texture is coarse, with visible grain and some areas that look slightly smoother due to erosion or wear. The coloration transitions from deep charcoal and slate tones on the main faces to lighter, dusty grays and browns at the base, possibly from contact with soil or concrete. This rock would be a substantial obstacle in an urban environment, likely used as a natural barrier or landscaping element.
description: A large, angular, dark gray rock with a rough, jagged surface and lighter weathered patches near the base. Its coarse texture and solid form make it a prominent obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: The rock displays a broad, slanted face with dark, shadowed areas and a rough, uneven lower edge.
description_view_1: This side reveals a flatter, more uniform surface with subtle textural variations and a pronounced ridge along the top.
description_view_2: The rock appears more triangular, with a sharp edge running from top to bottom and a mix of dark and light patches.
description_view_3: A more irregular, jagged profile is visible, with the base showing broken, lighter-colored stone and a deep crevice.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped roadside barrier
